channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Conservative activist reaches 'breaking point' with Google
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter delivers the latest developments form the world of artificial intelligence, including the technology's challenges and opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sora is even fooling human deepfake detectors
item.description: It used to be that artificial intelligence would leave behind helpful clues that an image it produced was not, in fact, real. Previous generations of the technology might give a person an extra finger or even an additional limb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Trump 'terribly advised' on crypto billionaire pardon: Key supporter
item.description: Venture capitalist Joe Lonsdale, a key supporter of President Trump, suggested Thursday that the president was “terribly advised” on his decision to pardon Binance founder Changpeng Zhao.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Lego just announced its official Black Friday sale - here are 11 early deals I'd buy right now
item.description: Lego’s own Black Friday deals event is officially returning for 2025, and with it, you can expect major discounts on sets that only Lego sells – but in the lead-up, you can save on these 12 deals from Lego and other retailers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The iPhone 18 Pro could come with a big Starlink upgrade – and it sounds perfect for off-grid adventures
item.description: We're starting to hear a few rumors around what Apple may have planned for the next set of flagship iPhones.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: EU accuses Meta and TikTok of breaching transparency rules
item.description: The European Union says Meta and TikTok have breached transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mcneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft has allegedly tasked Xbox to deliver a 30% profit margin, and if true, it could explain the reason behind its price hikes and mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox's job cuts and price increases could be due to a push from Microsoft to achieve a 30% profit margin.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Qué medidas pueden tomar los CIO para progresar en la era de la IA
item.description: Si le pregunta a cualquier director de TI sobre la IA, probablemente le dirá que es ideal para realizar tareas repetitivas y de bajo nivel.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Turning vision into value: The strategic role of intelligent IP cameras
item.description: The cameras and sensors that are installed across your business are doing more than watching. These IP cameras serve as a network of intelligent eyes across your operations, providing a potentially untapped resource that could improve business intelligence and operational efficiencies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From friendly text to financial trap: the new scam trend
item.description: Friendly text conversations about BBQs and social events can lead to WEEX gold trading scams that target older adults with fake investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would not deploy federal troops to San Francisco, reversing course on plans to implement anticrime and immigration enforcement in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Living in giant moon glass spheres could be our future
item.description: California company Skyeports creates self-healing glass spheres from Moon regolith that generate solar power and support plant growth for sustainable lunar living.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delete the fake VPN app stealing Android users' money
item.description: Cleafy researchers discover fake VPN streaming app Mobdro Pro that installs Klopatra banking Trojan, giving attackers full control over Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use keyed services in ASP.NET Core
item.description: Dependency injection (also known as DI) is a design pattern in which the dependent types of a class are injected (passed to it by another class or object) rather than created directly, thereby facilitating loose coupling and promoting easier testing and maintenance. In ASP.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Using the SkiaSharp graphics library in .NET
item.description: The news that the .NET UI framework Uno Platform project would be upstreaming features and fixes into the core multiplatform .NET libraries makes a lot of sense. The open source project has been working on cross-device .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission
item.description: Aligning tech, data and workforce to improve health outcomes starts with asking why, Maximus’ Monica Rosser says. The post Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Meghan Markle, Prince Harry join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: The couple is among more than 700 public figures to sign on to a brief statement by the Future of Life Institute.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management is looking to implement a commercial software-as-a-service human capital management system for all agencies by July 4, 2027. The post OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Google kills its cookie killer
item.description: Privacy Sandbox, Google’s attempt to create an alternative to cookies, looks like it has reached the end of the line. The company has announced that it is discontinuing 11 Privacy Sandbox technologies — pretty much the entire gamut.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Anthropic extends Claude Code to browsers
item.description: Anthropic has launched Claude Code on the web, enabling developers to use the company’s AI coding assistant directly from their browser or smartphone, with no terminal required. Developers can connect a first GitHub repository to Claude Code on the web at claude.com/code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less
item.description: "When you do any type of workforce reductions, you have to realize that you're also going to have attrition. Employees are going to leave," Rick Hammell said. The post With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network
